data.title: Pentair Is Maintained at Overweight by JP Morgan
data.description: (15:35 GMT) Pentair Price Target Cut to $124.00/Share From $132.00 by JP Morgan

data.title: Construction Labor Shortages Leading to Renewed Push for Prefab Building — Market Talk
data.description: Huge shortages in construction labor are increasing costs for builders and leading to a renewed push for modular or prefab construction to reduce labor costs, Jason Degnan-Rojeski, a managing director at Boston Consulting Group, tells WSJ. That could mean prehanging doors or putting together paneling that you can simply install at the site. Those changes do cause challenges including retraining workers. But "you're just trying to take construction off the job site," he says. "You are seeing this push basically [focused on] how can we take labor out?" (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

data.title: Treasury Yields March Higher
data.description:
The yield on the US 10-year Treasury note rose to 4.19% on Friday, its highest level in about two weeks, supported by strong economic data that reinforced the resilience of the US economy.

Industrial production increased by a faster-than-expected 0.4% last month, retail sales continued to point to robust consumer spending, and initial jobless claims signalled a resilient labour market, while price pressures linked to tariffs remained contained.

Looking ahead, next week’s PCE inflation report and GDP figures are expected to provide further insight into economic performance.

The Fed is widely expected to keep the federal funds rate unchanged at its upcoming meeting, with markets not pricing in a 25bps cut before June.

Meanwhile, geopolitical tensions with Iran have eased after President Trump signalled he was holding off on military strikes.

The US bond market will be closed on Monday for the Martin Luther King Jr.

Day holiday.
